subject: Breast
title: Gynaecomastia and the Drug Culprits
hook: Almost one in three men will get this at some point.

points.heading: Sky-high incidence
points.icon: a male chest with breast swelling
bullets: Driven by excess oestrogen or low androgens
Firm, mobile, concentric mass under the nipple

points.heading: The workup
bullets: Check breast, testicular and thyroid exam
Order beta-hCG, FSH, LH, testosterone and estradiol
Add testicular ultrasound if hormones are abnormal

points.heading: Drug culprits
bullets: Spironolactone and cimetidine are classic anti-androgens
Calcium channel blockers and ketoconazole are common triggers
Marijuana and anabolic steroids are often overlooked causes

points.heading: Watch and wait
bullets: Most cases regress within 6 to 24 months
Surgery is considered if no regression by 12 to 24 months
Persistent gynaecomastia after workup may need excision

captionLines: Almost 1 in 3 men will get this. 👀
Gynaecomastia comes from a shift toward more oestrogen or less androgen.
The drug list is a classic exam favourite so learn it cold.
Recall Engine turns any lecture into SBAs, MCQs, flashcards and high-yield notes so the drug cause list finally sticks.
